Job Title: Principal Capital Finance Business Partner | Perth
Ad Text:
About BHP 


At BHP we support our people to grow, learn, develop their skills and reach their potential. With a global portfolio of operations, we offer a diverse and inclusive environment with extraordinary career opportunities. Our strategy is to focus on creating a safe work environment where our employees feel strongly connected to our values and objectives, and where the capability of our people is key to our success.

Come and be a part of this success.

About the Role


The Principal Capital Finance Business Partner acts as a trusted advisor to the business, providing commercial insight, constructively challenging assumptions, and supporting informed decision making across the capital lifecycle. The role has a strong focus on capital productivity, governance, prioritisation, and ensuring alignment between strategic objectives and capital deployment.

Key Responsibilities:

Partnering with project and asset leaders to support capital planning, prioritisation, and performance management
Providing investment evaluation and commercial advice on capital projects
Stewardship of the capital portfolio, including cash prioritisation and governance
Driving improved capital efficiency and value outcomes through insight and challenge
Engaging across a broad range of stakeholders to support effective decision making
Leading and delivering capital reporting, including portfolio performance insights, cash flow, and capital productivity reporting, to support governance, prioritisation, and informed decision making

About You


To be successful in this role, you will be able to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders while maintaining a high attention to detail. You will be naturally curious with a continuous improvement mindset, inspiring challenge through highly effective communication and strong interpersonal skills. You will build constructive relationships and credibility across a range of stakeholders and cross functional teams.

Key experience and attributes:

Experience as a finance professional with strong commercial acumen and relevant finance exposure
Well developed commercial and analytical skills, including the ability to translate complex financial analysis into clear, actionable insights for decision makers
Demonstrated experience in reviewing capital investment, investment evaluation, or decision support, including providing commercial advice across the capital lifecycle and supporting disciplined capital allocation decisions
Postgraduate qualifications such as a Master’s degree or MBA, CPA, CFA, or CA are highly desirable
About Our Process 


At BHP, we are committed to employing individuals who align with the BHP Charter Values and meet the requirements of the role. As part of the recruitment process, there are a number of checks which may be conducted to demonstrate applicants suitability for a role including police / criminal background checks, medical, drug and alcohol testing, due diligence checks, right to work checks, and/or reference checks. 

If you are already employed directly by BHP, please log in using your BHP email address or apply via our internal jobs portal.
